item.title: Version 0.0.28
item.description: See the full release notes on GitHub.

item.title: Version 0.0.27
item.description: See the full release notes on GitHub.

item.title: Version 0.0.24
item.description:
• **Groq for cleanup.** Settings → Advanced → LLM Cleanup now has an OpenAI / Groq toggle, matching the existing Text Mode picker. Pick Groq, paste a `gsk-…` key, and cleanup runs on `llama-3.1-8b-instant` (fastest) or `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` (highest quality). Default stays OpenAI — opt in to switch.
  • **Latency stats.** A new card in Settings → Advanced shows mean ASR / LLM polish / paste / post-speech end-to-end across your last 10 dictations, so you can see where time is going and decide if a different model would help.
  • **Personal Dictionary in Settings.** Silent correction learning is now on by default; the captured corrections show up in Settings → Customization where you can review or remove them.
  • Every dictation also writes a `[Latency]` line to `/tmp/castword/debug.log` for diagnostics.

item.title: Version 0.0.23
item.description:
• **See your current version next to Check for Updates.** Settings now shows "Current version: 0.0.23 (build 23)" right beside the *Check for Updates…* button, so you can tell at a glance whether what Sparkle is offering is actually newer than what you're running.
  • Auto-update publish is now fully automated end-to-end — the appcast that drives Sparkle gets signed and pushed by the release pipeline directly, without a manual post-step.

item.title: Version 0.0.22
item.description:
• **Pick the model for each mode.** Text mode and Action mode now have their own model dropdowns in Settings (OpenAI + Groq, with pricing shown so you can see the trade-off).
  • **Context-aware transcription.** The dictation pass takes hints from the app you're in and your recent topics, so names and jargon you've used before come back correctly more often.
  • **Personalized formatting.** Per-app presets and a tone setting shape how your transcripts are punctuated and structured (e.g. terse code-comment style in your editor, full sentences in Slack).
  • **Audio-quality diagnostics.** A new in-app meter shows mic level + a one-line preflight check before recording, so silent-mic and wrong-device cases are caught up front.
  • **Correction learning.** When you edit a transcript, CastWord remembers the change and applies it next time — scoped per app with a loop guard so it can't drift into bad rules.
  • **Uncertainty signals.** Low-confidence words from the recogniser are flagged and the rare full-reject case is handled gracefully instead of silently producing garbage.
  • **Window-title sanitization.** Sensitive bits of window titles (filenames, URLs, etc.) are now redacted before being used as context, with a visible audit panel.
  • **Selection-context badge.** When CastWord uses your selected text for a command, a small badge shows what's being read.
  • **No more launch crash.** Resolved a SIGABRT (Swift exclusive-access violation) some users hit shortly after launch.
  • **Hotkey doesn't fire while you're rebinding it.** Capturing a new hotkey in Settings no longer triggers the existing one.
  • **API key from environment.** If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set in your environment, CastWord uses it on first launch instead of starting blank.

item.title: Version 0.0.21
item.description: • **Auto-update loop.** Sparkle's "An update is available" prompt no longer comes back after you install the latest version. (The build number stamped inside the app now matches the build number the appcast advertises, so Sparkle correctly recognises the update as installed.)

item.title: Version 0.0.20
item.description:
• **ESC cancels a recording.** Tap ESC while a hotkey is held and the in-progress recording is dropped — no transcription, no paste, nothing happens.
  • **Faster dictation.** Switched the transcription model to gpt-4o-mini-transcribe with streaming, so text appears noticeably sooner after you stop talking.
  • **Quieter Action Mode.** Tightened the Realtime prompt and stopped a class of duplicate tool-call firings that could happen right after a fast-path command (e.g. "open Safari" followed by a stray model-driven retry).
  • **Cleaner text mode.** The Stop button no longer appears during dictation, and the migration popup that used to interrupt some users on launch is suppressed.
  • **Status overlay on multi-monitor setups.** The overlay now shows on whichever screen your mouse cursor is on, instead of always pinning to the primary display.
  • **Corrections / snippets actually apply.** The text fixes you set in Settings (e.g. `teh = the`) now reach the cleanup pass instead of being silently dropped, and they're also fed to the LLM as soft hints.
